sections.label: Section 1.995
sections.translation: this godless , lawless , unjust , earth-born offspring of Echion .

sections.label: Section 1.997
sections.original: ὃς ἀδίκῳ γνώμᾳ παρανόμῳ τʼ ὀργᾷ
sections.translation: Whoever with wicked mind and unjust rage regarding your rites , Bacchus , and those of your mother , comes with raving heart

sections.label: Section 1.1000
sections.translation: and mad disposition violently to overcome by force what is invincible — death is the discipline for his purposes , accepting no excuses when the affairs of the gods are concerned ; to act like a mortal — this is a life that is free from pain . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.1005
sections.translation: I do not envy wisdom , but rejoice in hunting it . But other things are great and manifest . Oh , for life to flow towards the good , to be pure and pious day and night , and to honor the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1.1010
sections.translation: banishing customs that are outside of justice . Let manifest justice go forth , let it go with sword in hand , slaying through the throat

sections.label: Section 1.1015
sections.translation: this godless , lawless , unjust , earth-born offspring of Echion .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.translation: Go , Bacchus , with smiling face throw a deadly noose around the hunter of the Bacchae as he falls beneath the flock of Maenads .

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: ὦ δῶμʼ ὃ πρίν ποτʼ εὐτύχεις ἀνʼ Ἑλλάδα ,
sections.translation: Oh house once fortunate in Hellas ,

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.translation: house of the Sidonian old man who once sowed in the ground the earth-born harvest of the serpent Ophis , how I groan for you , though I am a slave , but still the masters ’ affairs are a concern to good servants . [*] .

sections.label: Section 1029
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; ἐκ βακχῶν τι μηνύεις νέον ;
sections.translation: What is it ? Do you bring some news from the Bacchae ?

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.translation: Pentheus , the child of Echion , is dead .

sections.label: Section 1031
sections.translation: Lord Bacchus , truly you appear to be a great god .

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; τί τοῦτʼ ἔλεξας ; ἦ ʼπὶ τοῖς ἐμοῖς
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Why have you said this ? Do you rejoice at the misfortunes of my master , woman ?

sections.label: Section 1034
sections.translation: I , a foreign woman , rejoice with foreign songs ;

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: οὐκέτι γὰρ δεσμῶν ὑπὸ φόβῳ πτήσσω .
sections.translation: for no longer do I cower in fear of chains .

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.translation: Do you think Thebes so lacking in men ?

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: ὁ Διόνυσος ὁ Διόνυσος , οὐ Θῆβαι
sections.translation: Dionysus , Dionysus , not Thebes , holds my allegiance .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: συγγνωστὰ μέν σοι , πλὴν ἐπʼ ἐξειργασμένοις
sections.translation: You may be forgiven , but still it is not good

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: κακοῖσι χαίρειν , ὦ γυναῖκες , οὐ καλόν .
sections.translation: to rejoice at troubles once they have actually taken place , women .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: ἔννεπέ μοι , φράσον , τίνι μόρῳ θνῄσκει
sections.translation: Tell me , speak , what kind of a death did he die , the unjust man who did unjust things ?

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.translation: When we left the dwellings of the Theban land and crossed the streams of Asopus ,

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.translation: we began to ascend the heights of Kithairon , Pentheus and I — for I was following my master — and the stranger who was our guide to the sight . First we sat in a grassy vale ,

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.translation: keeping our feet and voices quiet , so that we might see them without being seen . There was a little valley surounded by precipices , irrigated with streams , shaded by pine trees , where the Maenads were sitting , their hands busy with delightful labors . Some of them were crowning again

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.translation: the worn thyrsos , making it leafy with ivy , while some , like colts freed from the painted yoke , were singing a Bacchic melody to one another . And the unhappy Pentheus said , not seeing the crowd of women : Stranger ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.translation: from where we are standing I cannot see these false Maenads . But on the hill , ascending a lofty pine , I might view properly the shameful acts of the Maenads . And then I saw the stranger perform a marvelous deed . For seizing hold of the lofty top-most branch of the pine tree ,

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: κατῆγεν , ἦγεν , ἦγεν ἐς μέλαν πέδον ·
sections.translation: he pulled it down , pulled it , pulled it to the dark earth . It was bent just as a bow or a curved wheel , when it is marked out by a compass , describes a circular course [*] : in this way the stranger drew the mountain bough with his hands and bent it to the earth , doing no mortal ’ s deed .

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: Πενθέα δʼ ἱδρύσας ἐλατίνων ὄζων ἔπι ,
sections.translation: He sat Pentheus down on the pine branch , and let it go upright through his hands steadily , taking care not to shake him off . The pine stood firmly upright into the sky , with my master seated on its back .

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: ὤφθη δὲ μᾶλλον ἢ κατεῖδε μαινάδας .
sections.translation: He was seen by the Maenads more than he saw them , for sitting on high he was all but apparent , and the stranger was no longer anywhere to be seen , when a voice , Dionysus as I guess , cried out from the air : Young women ,

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ἄγω τὸν ὑμᾶς κἀμὲ τἀμά τʼ ὄργια
sections.translation: I bring the one who has made you and me and my rites a laughing-stock . Now punish him ! And as he said this a light of holy fire was placed between heaven and earth . 1084 The air became quiet and the woody glen

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: φύλλʼ εἶχε , θηρῶν δʼ οὐκ ἂν ἤκουσας βοήν .
sections.translation: kept its leaves silent , nor would you have heard the sounds of animals . But they , not having heard the sound clearly , stood upright and looked all around . He repeated his order , and when the daughters of Kadmos recognized the clear command of Bacchus ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.translation: they rushed forth , swift as a dove , running with eager speed of feet , his mother Agave , and her sisters , and all the Bacchae . They leapt through the torrent-streaming valley and mountain cliffs , frantic with the inspiration of the god .

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: ὡς δʼ εἶδον ἐλάτῃ δεσπότην ἐφήμενον ,
sections.translation: When they saw my master sitting in the pine , first they climbed a rock towering opposite the tree and began to hurl at him boulders violently thrown . Some aimed with pine branches and other women hurled their thyrsoi through the air

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: Πενθέως , στόχον δύστηνον · ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἤνυτον .
sections.translation: at Pentheus , a sad target indeed . But they did not reach him , for the wretched man , caught with no way out , sat at a height too great for their eagerness . Finally like lightning they smashed oak branches and began to tear up the roots of the tree with ironless levers .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ μόχθων τέρματʼ οὐκ ἐξήνυτον ,
sections.translation: When they did not succeed in their toils , Agave said : Come , standing round in a circle , each seize a branch , Maenads , so that we may catch the beast who has climbed aloft , and so that he does not make public the secret dances of the god . They applied countless hands

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.translation: to the pine and dragged it up from the earth . Pentheus fell crashing to the ground from his lofty seat , wailing greatly : for he knew he was in terrible trouble . 1114 His mother , as priestess , began the slaughter ,

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: καὶ προσπίτνει νιν · ὃ δὲ μίτραν κόμης ἄπο
sections.translation: and fell upon him . He threw the headband from his head so that the wretched Agave might recognize and not kill him . Touching her cheek , he said : It is I , mother , your son , Pentheus , whom you bore in the house of Echion .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: οἴκτιρε δʼ ὦ μῆτέρ με , μηδὲ ταῖς ἐμαῖς
sections.translation: Pity me , mother , and do not kill me , your child , for my sins . But she , foaming at the mouth and twisting her eyes all about , not thinking as she ought , was possessed by Bacchus , and he did not persuade her .

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.translation: Seizing his left arm at the elbow and propping her foot against the unfortunate man ’ s side , she tore out his shoulder , not by her own strength , but the god gave facility to her hands . Ino began to work on the other side ,

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: ῥηγνῦσα σάρκας , Αὐτονόη τʼ ὄχλος τε πᾶς
sections.translation: tearing his flesh , while Autonoe and the whole crowd of the Bacchae pressed on . All were making noise together , he groaning as much as he had life left in him , while they shouted in victory . One of them bore his arm , another a foot , boot and all . His ribs were stripped bare

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.translation: from their tearings . The whole band , hands bloodied , were playing a game of catch with Pentheus ’ flesh . His body lies in different places , part under the rugged rocks , part in the deep foliage of the woods , not easy to be sought . His miserable head ,

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.translation: which his mother happened to take in her hands , she fixed on the end of a thyrsos and carries through the midst of Kithairon like that of a savage lion , leaving her sisters among the Maenads ’ dances . She is coming inside these walls , preening herself

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.translation: on the ill-fated prey , calling Bacchus her fellow hunter , her accomplice in the chase , the glorious victor — in whose service she wins a triumph of tears . And as for me , I will depart out of the way of this calamity before Agave reaches the house .

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: τὸ σωφρονεῖν δὲ καὶ σέβειν τὰ τῶν θεῶν
sections.translation: Soundness of mind and reverence for the affairs of the gods is best ; and this , I think , is the wisest possession for those mortals who adopt it .

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.translation: Let us honor Bacchus with the dance , let us raise a shout for what has befallen

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.translation: Pentheus , descendant of the serpent , who assumed female attire and the wand , the beautiful thyrsos — certain death — and a bull was the leader of his calamity .

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.translation: Kadmean Bacchae , you have accomplished a glorious victory , but one that brings woe and tears . It is a noble contest to cover one ’ s dripping hands with the blood of one ’ s own son .

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἰσορῶ γὰρ ἐς δόμους ὁρμωμένην
sections.translation: But , for I see Pentheus ’ mother Agave coming home , her eyes contorted , receive the revel of the god of joy !

sections.label: Section 1.1168
sections.translation: Asian Bacchae —

sections.label: Section 1.1168b
sections.translation: Why do you excite me , oh ?

sections.label: Section 1.1169
sections.translation: I am bringing home from the mountain a

sections.label: Section 1.1170
sections.translation: freshly cut tendril to the house , blessed prey .

sections.label: Section 1.1172
sections.translation: I see it and will accept you as a fellow reveler .

sections.label: Section 1.1173
sections.translation: I caught this young wild lion cub without snares ,

sections.label: Section 1.1175
sections.translation: as you can see .

sections.label: Section 1.1176
sections.translation: From what desert ?

sections.label: Section 1.1177
sections.translation: Kithairon —

sections.label: Section 1.1177b
sections.translation: Kithairon ?

sections.label: Section 1.1178
sections.translation: Slew him .

sections.label: Section 1.1179
sections.translation: Who struck him ?

sections.label: Section 1.1179b
sections.translation: The honor is mine first .
